title.en: Setting Boundaries: "That's a bit difficult for me to say."
title.ko: 그건 말씀드리기 좀 곤란하네요 (선 넘는 질문에 대처하기)

description.en: Getting nosy questions? Learn how to protect your privacy gracefully using the professional and firm phrase "곤란하네요."
description.ko: 사생활을 묻거나 대답하기 난처한 질문을 받았을 때! 상대방의 기분을 상하지 않게 하면서도 내 선을 확실히 지키는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Workplace. Boundary. Privacy. Manners. Polite Refusal. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I had a bit of a culture shock today. A person I just met asked me exactly how much I pay for rent and if I'm planning to get married soon!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I didn't want to answer, but I didn't want to be rude either. In English, I'd say "That's personal," but that feels a bit too cold in Korean.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국에서도 요즘은 그런 사적인 질문을 조심스러워하는 추세지만 여전히 당황스러운 상황이 생기곤 하죠.
content.translation: Right. In Korea these days, people tend to be careful with such personal questions, but embarrassing situations still happen.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "곤란하다" 라는 단어를 써보세요. 아주 정중하면서도 확실하게 선을 그어주는 마법의 단어거든요.
content.translation: In those cases, try using the word "Gon-lan-hada." It's a magic word that draws a line very politely yet firmly.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "곤란하다"? Does it mean "difficult" or "awkward"?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 둘 다 맞아요. "대답하기 상황이 좀 어렵다" 라는 뜻이라서 상대방도 더 이상 묻지 못하게 만들죠.
content.translation: Both are correct. It means "The situation is a bit difficult to answer," so it makes the other person stop asking.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 내 사생활을 지키는 품격 있는 선긋기 표현을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn 'high-class boundary-setting' reactions to protect your privacy!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 말씀드리기 좀 곤란하네요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: This sounds much more professional than just saying "I don't want to talk about it."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 상대방을 탓하는 게 아니라 내 상황이 어렵다고 말하는 거니까요.
content.translation: Right! Because you're saying 'my situation' is difficult, not blaming the other person.

insight.title: 그건 말씀드리기 좀 곤란하네요
insight.description: 'That's a bit difficult/awkward for me to say'
insight.usage_tip: 말씀드리기(To tell/Honored) + 좀(A bit) + 곤란하네요(It is difficult/awkward). This is the best way to decline a question without being aggressive.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to delay the answer instead of saying a flat no?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "나중에 기회 되면 말씀드릴게요" 라고 웃으면서 말해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying with a smile, "I'll tell you when I get a chance later."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 지금은 말하고 싶지 않다는 의사를 아주 부드럽게 전달하는 방법이에요.
content.translation: It's a very soft way to convey that you don't want to speak right now.

insight.title: 나중에 기회 되면 말씀드릴게요
insight.description: 'I'll tell you later if there's a chance'
insight.usage_tip: This is a classic Korean social "exit phrase." It politely ends the topic without promising a specific time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm at a company dinner, and a nosy older colleague is asking about my personal finances.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 한국 생활은 할 만해요? 근데 월급 받아서 월세 내면 남는 게 좀 있어요? 저축은 많이 하나?
content.translation: John, is life in Korea doable? But after paying rent from your salary, is there anything left? Do you save a lot?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아! 걱정해 주셔서 감사합니다. 그래도 부족하지 않게 잘 지내고 있어요.
content.translation: Ah, thank you for worrying about me. I'm doing well without lacking much.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 에이~ 그래도 구체적으로 얼마 정도 모았는지 궁금하네! 나중에 집 살 때 도움 주려고 그러지!
content.translation: Ayy, but I'm curious exactly how much you've saved! I'm asking to help you when you buy a house later!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 개인적인 일이라 말씀드리기 좀 곤란하네요.
content.translation: Ah, that's a personal matter, so it's a bit difficult for me to say.

insight.title: 말씀드리기 좀 곤란하네요
insight.usage_tip: By adding '개인적인 일이라' (Because it's a personal matter), John gives a clear reason why he is drawing the line.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아... 그래요? 하긴 요즘 젊은 사람들은 그런 거 싫어하지. 내가 너무 실례했네!
content.translation: Ah... is that so? Well, young people these days don't like that. I was too rude!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요. 나중에 더 친해지면 말씀드릴게요!
content.translation: No, it's not. I'll tell you later when we get closer!

insight.title: 나중에 더 친해지면
insight.usage_tip: This shows you're not angry at the person, just the question.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, once I said the word "곤란하다," the person actually apologized! It’s a very powerful tool.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 정중하게 선을 긋는 건 무례한 게 아니라, 서로의 관계를 오래 지키기 위한 건강한 리액션이에요.
content.translation: Right. Drawing a line politely isn't being rude; it's a healthy reaction to protect the relationship for a long time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "boundary-setting" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 말씀드리기 좀 곤란하네요!
content.translation: That's a bit difficult for me to say!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 나중에 기회 되면 말씀드릴게요!
content.translation: I'll tell you later if there's a chance!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 제가 좀 개인적인 일이라서요!
content.translation: Because that's a bit of a personal matter for me!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 대답하기가 좀 난처하네요!
content.translation: It's a bit awkward to answer!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이해해 주셔서 감사합니다!
content.translation: Thank you for understanding!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 누군가의 질문이 불편하게 느껴진다면, 억지로 대답하지 말고 정중하게 여러분의 선을 보여주세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if someone's question feels uncomfortable, don't force an answer; try showing your line politely.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "그건 말씀드리기 좀 곤란하네요."
content.translation: That's a bit difficult for me to say.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.meaning: To be difficult / To be in a tough spot
vocabulary.example: 지금은 제가 대답하기 좀 곤란한 상황이에요. (Now is a bit of a difficult situation for me to answer.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be awkward / To be in a tight spot
vocabulary.example: 갑작스러운 부탁을 받아서 정말 난처했어요. (I was in a really tight spot because I received a sudden request.)
아무리 친해도 서로의 사생활은 존중해줘야 해요. (No matter how close you are, you should respect each other's privacy.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be rude / To excuse oneself
vocabulary.example: 질문이 너무 무례했다면 실례했습니다. (I'm sorry if my question was too rude.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be firm / To be resolute
vocabulary.example: 그는 거절할 때 아주 단호한 태도를 보였어요. (He showed a very firm attitude when refusing.)
상대방의 기분을 상하게 하는 건 선을 넘는 행동이에요. (Making the other person feel bad is an act of crossing the line.)
